answer:
**Several works in the Falsafa corpus reference specific named places such as cities (e.g., Athens, Rome, Alexandria, Thebes, Corinth), rivers (e.g., Nile), and regions (e.g., Gaul, Egypt, Persia, Sparta).**[^1][^2][^3][^4][^5][^6]

These primarily occur in Old English poetry (e.g., ancient nations and cities), Urdu poetry by Allama Iqbal (e.g., Islamic and classical historical sites), and French political-economic treatises by Charles Comte and Charles Dunoyer (e.g., classical antiquity for historical comparisons). The Indic smṛti texts (e.g., *Manusmṛti*, *Āṅgirasa Smṛti*) and most Kawi works yielded no such references in searches. Cynewulf's poems like *Andreas* and *Juliana* mention "heathens" but no specific places; *Elene* does reference Rome.[^7]

### Key works with references:
- **Old English Elegies** (*unknown-old-english-elegies-d5fe9e*): *Widsith* catalogs ancient peoples/regions including Israelites, Assyrians, Hebrews, Indians, Egyptians, Medes, Persians.[^8]
- **Bang-E-Dara** (parts 1–3; *allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-*-*): Rome (imperial might), Persia (kings, sun), Egypt (with Babylon), Cordoba (Islamic light), China/Yathrib.[^9][^10]
- **Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I** (*charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce*): Athens (slaves), Rome/Sparta/Greece (slavery/culture), Egypt/Gaul/Carthage.[^11]
- **Traité de Législation** (Vols. I–IV; *charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-*-*): Rome/Athens/Sparta (morals/slavery), Egypt/Nile/Alexandria/Thebes (social order), Gaul/Carthage/Corinth/Persia.[^12][^13][^14]
- **Traité de la propriété: VOL I** (*charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-i-2c7a99*): Gaul (tribes/territory), Philadelphia (modern contrast).[^15]
- **Elene** (*cynewulf-elene-d2d132*): Rome (bishop Eusebius).[^16]

No exhaustive list of every place exists without reading all chapters (~1,500+), but searches for 14+ terms (e.g., Troy yielded false positives on "destroy") confirm these works. The remaining ~20 works (mostly Indic legal texts) appear devoid of such references.

[^1]: *Charles Dunoyer, Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*, [ch. 7, p-6c6409](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/07-ii-liberte-compatible-avec-la-maniere-de-vivre-des/translation/#p-6c6409).
[^2]: *Charles Comte, Traité de Législation: VOL III*, [ch. 48, p-b496c9](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/48-de-la-superiorite-des-peuples-d-espece-caucasienne/translation/#p-b496c9).
[^3]: *Unknown, Old English Elegies*, [ch. 14, p-8276c0](/works/unknown-old-english-elegies-d5fe9e/14-widsith-matholade-wordhord-onleac/translation/#p-8276c0).
[^4]: *Allama Iqbal, Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, [ch. 5, p-b2df86](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/05-zamana-aaya-hai-be-hijabi-ka-aam-deedaar-e-yaar-ho/translation/#p-b2df86).
[^5]: *Charles Comte, Traité de Législation: VOL III*, [ch. 33, p-a2fa72](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/33-des-relations-observees-entre-l-aristocratie-milit/translation/#p-a2fa72).
[^6]: *Charles Dunoyer, Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*, [ch. 7, p-799941](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/07-ii-liberte-compatible-avec-la-maniere-de-vivre-des/translation/#p-799941).
[^7]: *Cynewulf, Elene*, [ch. 1, p-f5d1f1](/works/cynewulf-elene-d2d132/01/translation/#p-f5d1f1).
[^8]: *Unknown, Old English Elegies*, [ch. 14, p-8276c0](/works/unknown-old-english-elegies-d5fe9e/14-widsith-matholade-wordhord-onleac/translation/#p-8276c0).
[^9]: *Allama Iqbal, Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, [ch. 2, p-9bfa99](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-9bfa99).
[^10]: *Allama Iqbal, Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, [ch. 9, p-3af65d](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/09-goristan-e-shahi/translation/#p-3af65d).
[^11]: *Charles Dunoyer, Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*, [ch. 7, p-6c6409](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/07-ii-liberte-compatible-avec-la-maniere-de-vivre-des/translation/#p-6c6409).
[^12]: *Charles Comte, Traité de Législation: VOL II*, [ch. 1, p-b07df5](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/01-de-ce-qui-constitue-le-perfectionnement-et-la-degr/translation/#p-b07df5).
[^13]: *Charles Comte, Traité de Législation: VOL III*, [ch. 33, p-a2fa72](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iii-ca589a/33-des-relations-observees-entre-l-aristocratie-milit/translation/#p-a2fa72).
[^14]: *Charles Comte, Traité de Législation: VOL IV*, [ch. 5, p-795917](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iv-bca040/05-de-l-influence-de-l-esclavage-sur-l-existence-des-/translation/#p-795917).
[^15]: *Charles Comte, Traité de la propriété: VOL I*, [preface, p-d61219](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-i-2c7a99/00-preface/translation/#p-d61219).
[^16]: *Cynewulf, Elene*, [ch. 1, p-f5d1f1](/works/cynewulf-elene-d2d132/01/translation/#p-f5d1f1).
